objective: Check whether supported non-loading LM Studio CLI paths can resolve the retained LFM2.5-VL model despite `lms ls --json` not exposing it.

details: Ran `lms load --estimate-only` against the retained absolute model directory.
Inspected `lms import --help` and ran `lms import --dry-run` against the retained model.safetensors file with explicit user/repo metadata.
Did not load a model, import a model, edit LM Studio internals, or run live inference.

commands.stdout:
Model not found

No model found that matches model key "/Volumes/StudioStackSSD4TB/Development/LLM/lmstudio/lmstudio-community/LFM2.5-VL-1.6B-MLX-8bit".

To see a list of all downloaded models, run:

    lms ls

To select a model interactively, remove the --yes flag:

    lms load
commands.finding: LM Studio cannot resolve the retained absolute model directory through `lms load --estimate-only`.
`lms import` accepts a file path and supports `--dry-run`, but it is file-based rather than directory-based.

commands.stdout:
W The file name does not look like a model file. This may not work.
W Model files usually have extensions: .gguf
Error: Target file already exists: /Volumes/StudioStackSSD4TB/Development/LLM/lmstudio/lmstudio-community/LFM2.5-VL-1.6B-MLX-8bit/model.safetensors
commands.finding: The import dry-run does not provide a supported MLX directory registration path; it treats the safetensors file as suspicious/non-GGUF and errors.

decision.blocker: LM Studio cannot resolve the retained model via `lms ls --json`, `lms load --estimate-only <absolute-model-dir>`, or `lms import --dry-run <model.safetensors>`.
decision.next_action: Continue to use only the supported `lms get <hf-url> --mlx -y` registration/download path or a future LM Studio-supported model registration mechanism; do not hand-edit indexes or promote runtime work before preflight reports ready_for_live_validation=true.
